_comment: Phrasing templates for the Announcements feature. See dev/announcements.md §3.
Each bucket has a `balanced` list (always present, used by Balanced verbosity)
and an optional `chatty` list (longer / more conversational variants used when
the user picks Chatty). Minimal verbosity bypasses these templates and speaks
just `{name}.` directly. Translators rewrite natively, not literally.
Tone guide (revised v4 — sound like a birding companion, not a classifier):
- Minimal: bare species name only (handled by the engine).
- Balanced: short, natural, the way a friend walking next to you would
point a bird out. Never narrate the model ('solid match', 'good signal',
'first one this session') — that's the robotic tone we're avoiding.
- Chatty: a relaxed full sentence, occasionally a small warm aside. Hedge
like a person ('I think', 'hard to say'), not like software ('signal is
faint'). Keep it unhurried and understated.

balanced: That has the ring of a {name}.
chatty: I'd say that's a {name}, fairly sure.
That has a {name} feel to it.
Sounds like the {name} coming back around.
That'll be the {name} again, most likely.
Could be a {name} — hard to say.
Sounds a bit like a {name}, worth another listen.
Maybe a {name} in there — keep an ear out.
I'd guess {name}, but I'm not certain.
Might be a {name}, tricky one to call.
{name} would be my guess — take it with a pinch of salt.
Might be the {name} again — hard to be sure.
Could be that {name} coming back, tricky to tell.
Maybe the {name} once more, but I wouldn't swear to it.

balanced: Busy moment: {name1}, {name2}, {name3}, and a few more.
chatty: Lots going on — {name1}, {name2}, {name3}, and several more.
A real chorus — {name1}, {name2}, {name3}, and others besides.
Busy out there — {name1}, {name2}, {name3}, and a few more.
Plenty at once — {name1}, {name2}, {name3}, and more joining in.

_commonnessComment: Optional one-line tag-on appended (Chatty only) the *first* time a species
is announced in a session. Bin = the same distribution-adaptive abundance
tier shown for the species on the Explore screen. The seasonal addendum is
appended only when the species is also currently below its annual peak.
Keep these short — they follow a full sentence, so long clauses drag.

abundant: A really common one around here.
You'll hear this one a lot.
A familiar voice in these parts.
common: You're in good territory for it.
uncommon: Not one you hear every day here.
A bit less common around here.
Something of a treat in your area.
frequent: Heard pretty regularly in your area.
A frequent visitor in your area.
scarce: On the scarce side around here.
Not heard too often in your area.
You won't catch this one often around here.
Thin on the ground in your area.
rare: A real rarity here — nice one!
One to remember — rare in these parts.
seasonalAddendum: And a bit out of season, too.
Not usually around this time of year, either.
A touch early or late for it, normally.
Off its usual season here, which is interesting.
Outside its normal window here, too.
